role: Author; translator; Iranian Writers' Association member; assassinated 1998
bio: Mohammad Jafar Pouyandeh was a prominent author and translator who, along with Mohammad Mokhtari, served on the organising committee of the [Iranian Writers' Association](/orgs/iranian-writers-association). He was a signatory of the 1994 'We Are the Writers!' open letter demanding an end to censorship. On 8 December 1998 — just days after Mokhtari's disappearance — Pouyandeh vanished. His body was found strangled in the Shahriar district west of Tehran on 11 December. He and Mokhtari were the last known victims of the [chain murders](/events/chain-murders-iran-1998). The subsequent investigation traced both killings to the same [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence) unit. Lawyer [Nasser Zarafshan](/people/nasser-zarafshan) represented Pouyandeh's family; his wife Sima Sahebi was herself arrested for publishing a letter criticising the authorities for blocking memorial services.
editorial_notes: Born 1954. Translator and author. Iranian Writers' Association. Disappeared 8 December 1998, body found 11 December in Shahriar, strangled.
